filtered_articles.title: Russian Intelligence Services Continue to Target Commercial Messaging Applications
filtered_articles.summary: CISA and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) issued an updated Public Service Announcement (PSA) warning of Russian Intelligence Services (RIS) cyber threat actors targeting commercial messaging applications in ongoing phishing campaigns. This PSA is an update to the March 2026 Russian Intelligence Services Target Commercial Messaging Application Accounts and provides recent tactics, recommended mitigations, and samples of phishing messages.
filtered_articles._gpt_reason: Targeting of commercial messaging applications by Russian Intelligence

filtered_articles.title: US posts $10 million reward over Russian cyber campaign targeting Signal, WhatsApp
filtered_articles.summary: Russia-linked hacking groups tracked as UNC5792 and UNC4221 have socially engineered their way into the messaging accounts of government officials.

filtered_articles.title: Ukraine Says Russian Intelligence Used Fake Support Texts to Steal Messaging Credentials

filtered_articles.title: How to Detect SMS Blasters
filtered_articles.summary: SMS blasters operate outside legitimate mobile networks, but their activity can still be detected. This article explains how operators and regulators can identify false base stations by looking for radio, signalling and location anomalies. The post How to Detect SMS Blasters appeared first on Commsrisk.

filtered_articles.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
filtered_articles.summary: CISA has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog, based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-12569 PTC Windchill and FlexPLM Improper Input Validation Vulnerability CVE-2026-20230 Cisco Unified Communications Manager Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) Vulnerability These types of vulnerabilities are frequent attack vectors for malicious cyber actors and pose significant risks to the federal enterprise. filtered_articles.title: Webinar: Why business email compromise attacks keep succeeding
filtered_articles.summary: Business email compromise attacks increasingly rely on convincing impersonation rather than malware, making them harder for employees and traditional email defenses to detect. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help identify sophisticated email threats and automate response workflows. [...]

competitor_analysis.title: Network Volume Alerts for SMS
competitor_analysis.analysis: This feature automatically alerts users when SMS traffic on a network deviates from historical norms, enabling early detection of potential fraud. Customers benefit by being able to quickly identify and mitigate unusual activity, reducing the risk of financial loss and reputational damage. Compared to Twilio, if similar real-time anomaly detection isn't available, Vonage's feature could offer a competitive edge by providing proactive fraud prevention capabilities that enhance customer trust and security.
